info.title: Schedules Direct JSON API
info.description:
# Schedules Direct JSON API

Schedules Direct is a nonprofit TV listings / EPG data service. This API provides access to program guide data including lineups, schedules, program metadata, images, and celebrity information.

## Authentication

1. Hash your password using SHA1 (lowercase hex, 40 characters)
2. `POST /token` with username and hashed password to obtain a token
3. Include the token in subsequent requests via the `token` header or `?token=` query parameter
4. Tokens are valid for 24 hours from the first request in that period

## Client Workflow

1. Obtain token via `POST /token`
2. Check account status via `GET /status`
3. Search headends via `GET /headends`
4. Preview and add lineups via `GET /lineups/preview/{lineupId}` and `PUT /lineups/{lineupId}`
5. Get lineup channel mapping via `GET /lineups/{lineupId}`
6. Check schedule changes via `POST /schedules/md5`
7. Download schedules via `POST /schedules`
8. Download program details via `POST /programs`
9. Download images via `GET /image/{uri}`

## Caching Strategy

All responses include MD5 hashes. Clients should cache data locally and use MD5 comparisons to detect changes before downloading updates.

## Date/Time Format

All dates and times use ISO 8601 in UTC/Zulu time (suffix `Z`). No DST adjustments are made.

## Character Encoding

All requests and responses use UTF-8.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Account status, server health, and client version checks

tags.name: Available
tags.description: Discovery of available countries, services, and transmitters

tags.name: Lineups
tags.description: Headend discovery, lineup management, and channel mapping

tags.name: Programs
tags.description: Program metadata, descriptions, and language cross-references

tags.name: Schedules
tags.description: Schedule retrieval and change detection via MD5

tags.name: Images
tags.description: Image metadata and retrieval for programs, stations, and celebrities

post.description: This is the first call your client must make — almost all other endpoints require a token. Authenticates with username and SHA1-hashed password (lowercase hex, 40 characters). A token is valid for 24 hours from the time it was first granted. Subsequent calls with the same credentials during the 24-hour window return the existing token. A token becomes invalid when: (1) 24 hours elapse since it was first granted, or (2) a new token is explicitly requested via the `newToken` field. If the service is offline (code 3000), disconnect and retry in 30 minutes — a token may still be returned for later use. There is a limited number of token requests allowed per 24-hour period; once obtained, reuse the token until it expires.

password.description: SHA1 hex hash of the password (lowercase, 40 characters)

newToken.description: Set to true to force generation of a new token instead of returning the existing one. Default is false.

400.description: Authentication failure (expired account, invalid hash, invalid user, lockout, disabled account, too many logins, too many IPs)

get.description: Returns account details (expiration, max lineups), subscribed lineups, last data update time, notifications, and system status.

get.summary: Check if IP is blocked on load balancer
get.description: Checks whether the client IP is blocked. Rate limited to 100 calls per 24 hours (resets at 00:00Z).

get.summary: Check latest version of a registered client
get.description: Returns the latest known version for a registered client application.
Returns the list of available service types (countries, languages, DVB-S satellites, etc.) with their URIs.
Returns countries grouped by region, including postal code patterns for headend lookups.
Returns a list of language digraphs and their language names.
Returns a list of satellites available for DVB-S lineups.

get.summary: Get DVB-T transmitters for a country
get.description: Returns a map of transmitter names to lineup IDs for the specified country (DVB-T only).

get.summary: Get headends for a postal code
get.description: Returns available headends (cable, satellite, antenna, etc.) for the specified country and postal code.

parameters.name: postalcode
parameters.description: Postal code valid for the specified country

get.description: Returns a preview of channels in a lineup without adding it to the account.
Returns the full channel-to-stationID mapping, station details, and metadata for a lineup already added to the account. Use the `verboseMap: true` header for additional digital transport data.

parameters.name: verboseMap
parameters.description: Set to 'true' for additional data on digital transports (providerCallsign, logicalChannelNumber, matchType)

put.summary: Add a lineup to the account
put.description: Adds a lineup to the user's account. Limited to 6 adds per 24-hour period. Default maximum of 4 lineups per account (can be increased via support).

delete.summary: Remove a lineup from the account
delete.description: Removes a lineup from the user's account.

get.summary: Get all lineups on the account
get.description: Returns all lineups currently added to the user's account.

post.description: Returns detailed metadata for up to 5000 program IDs per request.
Returns short (100-char) and long (1000-char) generic descriptions for EP-type program IDs. The response keys are the corresponding SH-prefix base IDs. Max 500 per request.

200.description: Descriptions returned, keyed by SH-prefix base programID

post.description: Returns available language versions of the specified program IDs.
Returns program schedules for the specified station IDs and optional date ranges. Max 5000 station IDs per request.

post.summary: Get schedule MD5 hashes for change detection
post.description: Returns MD5 hashes and last-modified timestamps for schedules, enabling clients to detect changes without downloading full schedules. Max 5000 station IDs per request.

200.description: MD5 hashes returned, keyed by stationID then date

get.summary: Check if a sports event is still in progress
get.description: Returns the live status and current score for an in-progress sporting event. Supports NFL, NHL, NBA, and MLB.

parameters.name: programId
parameters.description: SP-type programID for the sporting event

get.summary: Get image metadata for a single program
get.description: Returns available image metadata (URIs, dimensions, categories) for a single program ID or rootId. To receive episode-specific artwork (hasEpisodeArtwork), movie artwork (hasMovieArtwork), or sports artwork (hasSportsArtwork), send the full 14-character programID. Sending only the left-most 10 characters is deprecated and will return series-level artwork only. The response may include a rootId field that can be used to retrieve related artwork via this same endpoint. URIs in the response are ephemeral — do not store them permanently.

parameters.name: programId
parameters.description: 14-character programID, or a numeric rootId from a previous image metadata response

post.summary: Get image metadata for multiple programs (batch)
post.description: Returns image metadata for up to 500 program IDs. Use full 14-character program IDs — sending the left-most 10 characters is deprecated and will return only series-level artwork, not episode-specific images. Each element in the response is JSON, but note that when errors occur (e.g., invalid programID), the error response for that element is inline alongside successful results. URIs in the response are ephemeral — do not store them permanently.

get.description: Redirects (HTTP 303) to an S3 URL for the requested image. The redirect URL is valid for 120 seconds — do not cache it. Token can be provided via header or `?token=` query parameter. If the URI from the image metadata is already a complete S3 URL (contains 'https://'), access it directly instead of using this endpoint. There is a daily image download limit per subscriber (counter resets at 00:00Z). Exceeding it returns code 5002/5003 — stop requests immediately or the account will be blocked.

parameters.name: uri
parameters.description: Image URI from metadata response (SHA256 hash filename)

303.description: Redirect to S3 image URL (valid for 120 seconds)

Location.description: Temporary S3 URL for the image

404.description: Image not found (code 5000, do not retry)

get.description: Returns available images for a celebrity by their personId. The personId is preferred over nameId — a person may have multiple nameIds (due to marriage, alternate spellings, etc.) but a single personId. Response may include rootId and title fields linking images back to specific programs. URIs in the response are ephemeral — do not store them permanently. Note: the Retrieving Images wiki page indicates token is not required for this endpoint, while the main API page indicates it is required. Behavior may vary — send the token if available.

parameters.name: personId
parameters.description: Numeric person ID (as string, do not convert to integer)

get.description: Returns full biographical data for a celebrity including names, mediography, awards, and images. PersonId values must be treated as strings, not integers.

parameters.name: personId
parameters.description: Numeric person ID (as string, do not convert to integer)

delete.description: Removes a non-system message from the account status response.

TokenHeader.name: token
TokenHeader.description: Authentication token obtained from POST /token. Alternatively, pass as `?token=` query parameter (query parameter takes precedence).

code.description: Numeric error code. Ranges: 1xxx = format/request, 2xxx = parameter/lineup/station, 3xxx = service, 4xxx = account/auth, 5xxx = image, 6xxx = program, 7xxx = schedule, 9999 = unknown.

serverTime.description: Current server time as UNIX epoch timestamp

token.description: May be returned even during SERVICE_OFFLINE for later use

tokenExpires.description: Token expiration as UNIX epoch timestamp (may be 0 during SERVICE_OFFLINE)

retryTime.description: For SCHEDULE_QUEUED: honor this time before retrying

accountExpiration.description: UNIX epoch timestamp of account expiration (for ACCOUNT_EXPIRED)

eventStartDateTime.description: For FUTURE_PROGRAM: when the event is scheduled to start

programID.description: For PROGRAMID_QUEUED / FUTURE_PROGRAM: the programID in question

stationID.description: For SCHEDULE_QUEUED: the stationID being generated

minDate.description: For SCHEDULE_RANGE_EXCEEDED: earliest available date for the stationID

maxDate.description: For SCHEDULE_RANGE_EXCEEDED: latest available date for the stationID

requestedDate.description: For SCHEDULE_RANGE_EXCEEDED: the out-of-range date that was requested

tokenExpires.description: Token expiration as UNIX epoch timestamp

serverTime.description: Current server time as UNIX epoch timestamp

maxLineups.description: Maximum number of lineups allowed on the account

isDeleted.description: Only present if the lineup has been deleted

postalCode.description: Regex pattern for valid postal codes

onePostalCode.description: If true, the entire country uses a single postal code

Headend.description: A headend may contain multiple lineups. A lineup is a specific mapping of channels and stationIDs. For example, a Cable headend may offer: a DEFAULT lineup (analog channels 2-125, no set-top-box required), an X lineup (digital, requires set-top-box), and a QAM lineup (digital content available via QAM tuner without a set-top-box).

matchType.description: Verbose mode: how the channel was matched

logo.description: Legacy single logo object. Use stationLogo array instead.

category.description: Background color category the logo is designed for. May be null for legacy logos.

modulation.description: Present on QAM lineups to differentiate from standard Cable transport

changesRemaining.description: Number of lineup changes remaining in the current period

programID.description: 14-character program identifier. Prefixes: EP (episode), SH (show/series), MV (movie), SP (sport)

contentAdvisory.description: Content advisory labels (e.g., Adult Language, Violence)

hasImageArtwork.description: End-of-life as of API-20141201. Will not be included in the next API version. Replaced by hasSeriesArtwork (SH-level artwork), hasEpisodeArtwork (episode-specific stills/guest stars), hasMovieArtwork (movie posters/stills), and hasSportsArtwork (sports event imagery). These more-specific booleans are included in current responses for forward compatibility.

hash.description: 32-character standard MD5 hash (will become mandatory in future API versions)

titleLanguage.description: Language tag (e.g., 'en', 'en-GB'). Currently optional, will become mandatory.

nameId.description: Stringified numeric ID (do not convert to integer)

personId.description: Stringified numeric ID (do not convert to integer)

ProgramMetadata.description: Key-value object. Known keys: 'Gracenote' and 'TVmaze' with season/episode numbering.

totalEpisodes.description: For EP programs: total episodes in this season. For SH programs: total episodes in the series.

totalSeasons.description: Total number of seasons in the series. SH programs only.

code.description: 0 = OK, 6000 = invalid, 6001 = valid but not ready

date.description: Specific dates to request (YYYY-MM-DD). Omit to get all available dates.

code.description: Error code if this stationID had an issue (e.g., 2201 STATIONID_DELETED, 7020 SCHEDULE_RANGE_EXCEEDED, 7100 SCHEDULE_QUEUED)

retryTime.description: For SCHEDULE_QUEUED: wait until this time before retrying

requestedDate.description: For SCHEDULE_RANGE_EXCEEDED: the out-of-range date requested

new.description: True if this is a new episode

free.description: Normally paid content available free in this instance

code.description: Error code if there were issues with the station (e.g., 2201 for deleted stationID)

isDeleted.description: True if the stationID has been deleted upstream

ProgramImage.description: Image metadata returned from the program image index endpoints. Width/height may be integers (newer responses) or stringified integers (legacy responses).

width.description: Image width in pixels. May be integer or stringified integer.

height.description: Image height in pixels. May be integer or stringified integer.

uri.description: Image URI. If a complete S3 URL, access directly. If a relative path (e.g., 'assets/...'), use with GET /image/{uri} which returns a 303 redirect.

primary.description: If 'true', this is the primary image for the category

rootId.description: Root ID that can be used with GET /metadata/programs/{rootId} to retrieve related artwork

CelebrityImage.description: Image object returned by GET /metadata/celebrity/{personId}. Note: uses 'lastUpdated' (with 'd'). The embedded images array in GET /celebrity/{personId} uses 'lastUpdate' (without 'd') — see CelebrityDetailImage.

uri.description: Image URI. Use with GET /image/{uri} to retrieve.

rootId.description: Root ID linking to the program this image is associated with. Can be used with GET /metadata/programs/{rootId}.

title.description: Title of the program this image is associated with

lastUpdated.description: Field name is 'lastUpdated' (with trailing 'd') on this endpoint

CelebrityDetailImage.description: Image object embedded in the GET /celebrity/{personId} response. Note: uses 'lastUpdate' (without 'd'), unlike CelebrityImage which uses 'lastUpdated'.

lastUpdate.description: Field name is 'lastUpdate' (without trailing 'd') on this endpoint

personId.description: Stringified numeric ID (do not convert to integer)

nameId.description: Stringified numeric ID (do not convert to integer)
